text: <par>Initial <reconstruction>*k-</reconstruction> reanalyzed as prefix, dropped and replaced with <latinform>t(h)</latinform>, similar to Karen PIG.</par>

a) Probable Indic loan: nāga
b) The initial hyphen is spurious.

<par>Pa-O <latinform>khɔ̀k</latinform> likely a borrowing from Burmese <latinform>khauk</latinform> 'skin'. Pa-O has glottal stop as the regular reflex of all proto-Karen final stops. Pa-O final oral stops occur largely (though not entirely) in loans from Shan and Burmese.</par>
